findings.evidence: PR lines 70, 74, 78, 83, 110, 151, 190, 228 add `[DecidableEq ...]`; Mathlib has `Finsupp.single` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Finsupp/Single.lean:47` with only `[Zero M]`, plus `single_eq_same` at line 76 and `support_single` at line 103 without a `DecidableEq α` assumption.
findings.fix: Drop `[DecidableEq X]`/`[DecidableEq Y]` from `ofPoint`, `coeff_ofPoint_self`, `coeff_ofPoint_of_ne`, `support_ofPoint`, `isEffective_ofPoint`, `pushForward_ofPoint`, `degree_ofPoint`, and `weightedDegree_ofPoint`, using the existing `Finsupp.single_eq_same`, `Finsupp.single_eq_of_ne`, and `Finsupp.support_single` API where needed.
findings.issue: `ofPoint` and the downstream point-divisor lemmas require `[DecidableEq X]`, but this assumption is not needed for `Finsupp.single` or its basic coefficient/support lemmas.

summary: The point-divisor API is unnecessarily restricted to decidable equality on the point type. Remove those assumptions so the formal-sum layer works at the same generality as `Finsupp.single`.
